pattern: Reaching node 0 from anywhere only ever needs one chosen outgoing edge per node, so the smallest workable cap on edge weight is exactly the bottleneck (minimax) value of the cheapest-worst-edge path from each node back to the root -- a widest-path problem solved like Dijkstra but minimizing the maximum edge instead of the sum.
hints: Reverse every directed edge conceptually: you care about how each node can reach node 0, so build an adjacency list keyed by the edge's destination pointing back at its source.

Because you only ever need one surviving outgoing edge per node to form a path to the root, this reduces to: for every node, find a path to 0 whose largest edge weight is as small as possible -- a minimax shortest path, solvable with a Dijkstra-style relaxation that propagates max(currentBest, edgeWeight) instead of a sum.

Run the minimax relaxation from node 0 outward over the reversed edges; if every node ends up with a finite bottleneck value, the answer is the largest of those values, otherwise some node can never reach 0 and the answer is -1.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, one edge removed to reach weight 1
no edge points into node 0 at all, so nothing can reach it
a chain-like graph funneling into node 0 through node 4
one node has no outgoing edge at all and can never reach 0
smallest possible graph, two nodes, single edge into the root
two different edges from the same node to the same target with different weights, cheaper one should win
a star graph where every other node points directly at the root with varying weights
a longer path forces the bottleneck to be the largest single edge along it, not the sum

stress.description: a random tree of directed edges pointing toward 0 (guaranteed reachable) plus extra random shortcut edges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 1500))
    edges = []
    seen_pairs = set()
    for i in range(1, n):
        parent = rng.randint(0, i - 1)
        w = rng.randint(1, 1000000)
        edges.append([i, parent, w])
        seen_pairs.add((i, parent))
    extra = min(n, 500)
    for _ in range(extra):
        a = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        b = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        if a == b or (a, b) in seen_pairs:
            continue
        w = rng.randint(1, 1000000)
        edges.append([a, b, w])
        seen_pairs.add((a, b))
    threshold = rng.randint(1, n - 1)
    return [n, edges, threshold]
stress.note: every node has a guaranteed path to 0 via the generated tree, so this never produces an unreachable -1 case

stress.description: a random graph with some nodes deliberately cut off from node 0
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(4, min(size, 800))
    half = n // 2
    edges = []
    seen_pairs = set()
    for i in range(1, half):
        parent = rng.randint(0, i - 1)
        w = rng.randint(1, 1000000)
        edges.append([i, parent, w])
        seen_pairs.add((i, parent))
    for i in range(half, n):
        parent = rng.randint(half, i - 1) if i > half else half
        w = rng.randint(1, 1000000)
        if i != parent and (i, parent) not in seen_pairs:
            edges.append([i, parent, w])
            seen_pairs.add((i, parent))
    threshold = rng.randint(1, n - 1)
    return [n, edges, threshold]
stress.note: the second half of the nodes forms its own component disconnected from 0, forcing -1
